title: Blueprints
knowledge_note: Every great mosque was planned before it was built. Al-Jazari drew blueprints for machines 800 years ago. A blueprint in ILMA describes what something is and can do.
explanation: A 'blueprint' defines a type of thing — its data and its abilities. You then create instances from the blueprint, each with their own values.
starter_code:
blueprint Person:
    create(name, age):
        me.name = name
        me.age = age
    recipe introduce():
        say "I am " + me.name + ", age " + me.age

remember p = Person("Yusuf", 15)
p.introduce()
worked_example:
blueprint Person:
    create(name, age):
        me.name = name
        me.age = age
    recipe introduce():
        say "I am " + me.name + ", age " + me.age

remember p = Person("Yusuf", 15)
p.introduce()
challenge: Add a "city" field and mention it in introduce().
hint: Add me.city = city to create(), then add city to the say line.
